---

## About This Project

Bop exists to make senior-level code review universally available — on every PR, every round, regardless of team size or review backlog.

When Bop succeeds, a solo developer working with an AI assistant gets the same quality feedback loop as an engineer at a well-staffed company with dedicated security, architecture, and performance reviewers. A small team shipping fast doesn't have to choose between review quality and velocity. An enterprise team gets consistent, configurable review standards applied uniformly across every contribution.

Bop doesn't replace human judgment. It ensures that by the time a human looks at a PR — or a developer merges their own work — the obvious and not-so-obvious problems have already been surfaced, deduplicated, and ranked by confidence. The bar is raised before anyone has to raise it manually.

## Development Notes

### build
- Always prefer mage commands over raw go commands. Mage targets ensure consistent builds with version injection and proper flags. Key targets: mage build (bop binary), mage buildMCP (bop-mcp binary), mage buildAll (both), mage test, mage testRace, mage testCoverage, mage testUnit, mage testVerbose, mage format, mage lint, mage lintFix, mage vet, mage ci (full CI: format+lint+test-race+build-all), mage check (quick pre-commit: format+vet+test), mage all (everything), mage deps, mage tidy, mage generate, mage install, mage clean. Fallback raw go commands: go build -o bop ./cmd/bop, go build -o bop-mcp ./cmd/bop-mcp, go test ./..., go test -race ./..., gofmt -w ., golangci-lint run. (2026-03-17)

### documentation
- Additional documentation beyond the core docs: Design docs: docs/design/ (active design docs including 02-ARCHITECTURE.md). Archive: docs/archive/ (44 historical docs — old TDDs, roadmaps, phase designs, implementation plans). Security: docs/SECURITY.md (security model and threat analysis). GitHub Action Setup: docs/GITHUB_ACTION_SETUP.md (action integration guide). Configuration: docs/CONFIGURATION.md (config file reference). Context: docs/context/ (Telesis-managed context). (2026-03-17)

### rules
- Common pitfalls: (1) Don't skip tests — TDD is mandatory. (2) Don't import domain from adapters — clean architecture violation. (3) Don't commit secrets — redaction exists but prevention is better. (4) Don't use raw go commands — use mage targets instead. (5) Don't ignore the race detector — mage testRace must pass. (6) Don't ignore lint errors — fix ALL errors you find, even pre-existing ones. (2026-03-17)
- Definition of Done checklist: (1) Tests written (TDD). (2) Code formatted (mage format). (3) Lint passes (mage lint) — fix ALL errors, not just yours. (4) All tests pass (mage test). (5) Build succeeds (mage buildAll). (6) No race conditions (mage testRace). (2026-03-17)
- Core development rules: (1) TDD is mandatory — write tests first. (2) Clean Architecture — domain has no external dependencies. (3) Functional style — prefer immutability, SOLID principles. (4) Always use mage — prefer mage commands over raw go commands. (5) Fix ALL lint errors — fix any lint errors you encounter, even if you didn't cause them. This codebase accumulated technical debt from ignoring pre-existing issues; the only way to recover is to fix issues as you find them. (2026-03-17)

### skills
- Claude Code skills for targeted context loading: /skill development (building, testing, debugging), /skill github-workflow (commits, PRs, issues), /skill architecture (design questions, understanding codebase), /skill review (using the code reviewer itself), /skill triage-pr-review (triage PR code review feedback and SARIF alerts). Use these skills for targeted context instead of reading docs manually. (2026-03-17)

### status
- Project status: v0.12.3 stable. Phase 3.5 (Local Mode) is complete. Key completed phases: Phase 1 (Foundation) — multi-provider LLM, local CLI, basic GitHub workflow. Phase 2 (GitHub Native) — first-class reviewer with inline annotations. Phase 3.1 (Triage) — MCP server for AI-assisted triage. Phase 3.2 (Personas) — specialized reviewer roles. Phase 3.5 (Local Mode) — on-demand PR review, session storage, MCP review tools. Phase 3.3 (Dynamic Model Selection) was deferred. Interactive TUI (Bubble Tea) was planned but never implemented and is effectively dropped. (2026-03-17)

### workflow
- PR Triage Protocol: Use bop MCP server tools for triage. The server exposes tools in two categories. Reading: list_annotations (SARIF), get_annotation, list_findings (PR comments), get_finding, get_thread, get_code_context, get_diff_context, get_suggestion. Actions: reply_to_finding, post_comment, mark_resolved, request_rereview, edit_finding, post_findings. Triage workflow: (1) List findings via list_annotations + list_findings (always check both). (2) Analyze via get_finding, get_code_context, get_suggestion. (3) Fix valid findings in code. (4) Respond via reply_to_finding for PR comments, post_comment for SARIF. (5) Resolve via mark_resolved. (6) Re-review via request_rereview after fixes are pushed. (2026-03-17)
- Running reviews: ./bop review branch main (review current branch against main), ./bop review branch main --reviewers security,architecture (with specific personas), ./bop review branch main --output ./review-output (with output directory), ./bop review branch main --instructions 'Focus on security' (with custom context). GitHub Action usage: uses: delightfulhammers/bop/action@v0.12.3 with anthropic-api-key from secrets. (2026-03-17)
